items.title: Partial government shutdown looms as Congress leaves town without a deal
items.description: Large swaths of the Department of Homeland Security are set to shut down Saturday unless lawmakers and the White House strike a last-minute deal.

items.title: Justice Dept’s head of antitrust departs amid tensions on enforcement
items.description: Gail Slater — an antitrust attorney who vowed to resist political interference at her confirmation hearing — announced her departure on social media.

items.title: Judge rejects Hegseth bid to punish Sen. Kelly for video message to troops
items.description: The Trump administration intends to appeal the ruling, which says the retired Navy officer’s right to free speech was under attack.

items.title: Ocasio-Cortez, Rubio to offer dueling visions of world order in Munich
items.description: The potential 2028 presidential candidates will sound off on a range of burgeoning crises as European leaders recoil at America’s populist politics.

items.title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
items.description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington ...
